title: Consistent hashing — an interactive explainer
body_preview: Research & Learning · concept explainer Consistent hashing, in one ring You have K keys spread across N cache servers. A server dies, or you add one. How many keys have to move? With naive hash(key) mod N the answer is "almost all of them." Consistent hashing gets it down to roughly K /&nbs
